title: WISCONSIN DHS - BADGERCARE / CONTROLLED SUBSTANCE RECORDS

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Wisconsin Department of Health Services
Division of Medicaid Services -- Office of Inspector General
1 West Wilson Street
Madison, WI 53703

Re: Open Records Request -- BadgerCare Program Integrity

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39, I request:

1. Any fraud referrals or program integrity investigations regarding BadgerCare / Medicaid benefits for Tanya Marie Anderson (DOB: 10/15/1983) for the period 2019-2025.

2. Any records of referral to or from the Prescription Drug Monitoring Program (PDMP) regarding Tanya Marie Anderson.

3. Any records of cross-referral between the FoodShare IPV investigation conducted by Angela Dempewolf (resulting in the December 20, 2024 IPV finding, DHA Case No. FOF-215399) and BadgerCare / Medicaid program integrity.

4. Whether the IPV finding of concealed income triggered any review of BadgerCare eligibility or benefits received by Tanya Marie Anderson.

I am not requesting protected health information or medical records. I am requesting program integrity and fraud investigation records, which are agency administrative records subject to the open records law. The December 20, 2024 IPV finding confirmed over $52,917 in concealed income, which would affect BadgerCare eligibility thresholds.

NOTE: This request targets BadgerCare / Medicaid program integrity records specifically. A separate companion request has been filed with DHS OIG for the FoodShare/SNAP investigation file.

If any portion of this request is denied, I request a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption relied upon, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(b).

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(a). Fee waiver requested on public interest grounds.

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon

April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Wisconsin Department of Safety and Professional Services
4822 Madison Yards Way
Madison, WI 53705

Re: Open Records Request -- Dr. Donna A. Rifkin, Ph.D.

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39, I request:

1. The current license status, license number, and any restrictions or conditions on the license of Donna A. Rifkin, Ph.D. (psychologist), practicing in the Dane County, Wisconsin area.

2. Any formal complaints filed against Dr. Rifkin with DSPS or the Psychology Examining Board, including the date filed, general nature of the complaint, and disposition (dismissed, investigated, referred for discipline, pending).

3. Any completed disciplinary actions, orders, or stipulations involving Dr. Rifkin.

4. Any correspondence between DSPS and complainants regarding complaints about Dr. Rifkin, to the extent such records are disclosable under the open records law.

Dr. Rifkin is identified as an investigative target in legal proceedings for professional report inconsistencies, exclusion of a parent from treatment decisions, and evolving clinical notes that align with coached allegations rather than independent clinical assessment.

If any portion of this request is denied, I request a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption relied upon, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(b).

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(a). Fee waiver requested on public interest grounds.

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Office of Lawyer Regulation
110 East Main Street, Suite 315
Madison, WI 53703

Re: Open Records Request Pursuant to Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39 and SCR 21.10

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39, and SCR 21.10 (records of the Office of Lawyer Regulation), I request:

1. Any complaints filed against Assistant District Attorney Annie Jay (Dane County) and/or any records of OLR review, investigation, or disposition concerning her conduct in Dane County Circuit Court cases 2023CF001512, 2023CF002141, or 2023FA000836.

2. Any complaints filed against Assistant District Attorney Timothy Verhoff (Dane County) and/or any records of OLR review, investigation, or disposition, including any relating to an undisclosed conflict of interest arising from his prior representation of Tracy Henriksen in a 2011 criminal defense matter.

3. Whether any other attorney's conduct in the above-referenced cases has been the subject of an OLR complaint or referral.

I understand that certain OLR proceedings are confidential under SCR 22.40. To the extent any responsive records exist that are not subject to that confidentiality provision, I request their disclosure. If this request is denied in whole or in part, I request a written statement of the legal basis for denial, including the specific SCR or statutory provision relied upon.

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay. Fee waiver requested on public interest grounds.

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Wisconsin Department of Justice
Division of Criminal Investigation
Public Integrity Unit
17 West Main Street
Madison, WI 53703

Re: Open Records Request Pursuant to Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39, I request copies of the following records:

1. Any referrals received by the DOJ Public Integrity Unit from Dane County agencies (including but not limited to the District Attorney's Office, Department of Human Services, Department of Children and Families, and Waunakee Police Department) concerning any of the following individuals or cases:
   - Tanya Marie Anderson
   - Alexander Liam Lennon
   - Case Nos. 2023FA000836, 2023CF001512, 2023CF002141, 2024CF000722

2. Any complaints received regarding alleged misconduct by any of the following Dane County public officials:
   - ADA Annie Jay
   - ADA Timothy Verhoff
   - Officer Mitchell Flora (WPD)
   - Detective Jason Carbon (WPD)
   - CPS Investigator Heather Stertz
   - CPS Investigator Angela Ivan
   - DHS Employee Jessica Cooper

3. The current status of any such referrals or complaints (open, closed, declined, or referred to another agency).

4. Any records of the DOJ declining a referral from Dane County concerning any of the above matters, including the stated reason for declination.

I am not requesting details of active investigative techniques or confidential informant identities. I am requesting the existence and disposition of referrals and complaints, which are public records.

If any portion of this request is denied, I request a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption relied upon, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(b).

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(a). Fee waiver requested on public interest grounds.

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Wisconsin Department of Workforce Development
Division of Unemployment Insurance
P.O. Box 7905
Madison, WI 53707

Re: Open Records Request -- Employer Records Pursuant to Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39, I request copies of the following records:

PART A -- KOSA SPA, LLC

1. Employer registration and UI tax account records for KOSA SPA, LLC (principal: Shilpa Sankaran), including date of registration, employer ID number, and status.

2. Any records of employment verification requests received by DWD from any agency (including DHS) regarding employees of KOSA SPA LLC for the period 2019-2024, and KOSA's responses thereto.

3. Any fraud referrals, investigations, or determinations in which KOSA SPA LLC is identified as an employer that facilitated, enabled, or participated in benefit fraud by any employee or claimant.

4. Any correspondence between DWD and KOSA SPA LLC regarding employment status verification, wage reporting, or benefit eligibility of any individual claiming UI benefits while employed by KOSA.

PART B -- GIERINGER LAW OFFICE

5. Employer registration and UI tax account records for Gieringer Law Office and/or Mark Gieringer (407 Main St, Friendship, WI 53934; residence: 298 County Road M, Coloma, WI 54930), including registration status and employer ID.

6. Any employment verification requests sent to or received from Gieringer Law Office regarding Tanya Marie Anderson for the period 2019-2024.

7. Any records of DWD contact with Gieringer Law Office in connection with any UI claim, fraud investigation, or benefit eligibility determination.

8. Any wage reports filed by Gieringer Law Office for the period 2019-2024.

This request concerns two employers' public-facing registration records and their roles in a documented benefit fraud scheme confirmed by a December 20, 2024 DHA Intentional Program Violation finding (FOF-215399). Forensic accounting documents $153,209.68 in deposits from Gieringer Law Office to the benefit recipient over 2019-2023, during which period the recipient claimed unemployment benefits. I request a fee waiver based on public interest.

If any portion of this request is denied, I request a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption relied upon, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(b).

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(a).

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Wisconsin Department of Workforce Development
Division of Unemployment Insurance
Bureau of Benefit Operations / Program Integrity
P.O. Box 7905
Madison, WI 53707

Re: Open Records Request Pursuant to Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to the Wisconsin Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39, I request copies of the following records:

PART A -- FRAUD REPORT DISPOSITION

1. All records pertaining to my fraud report filed November 26, 2024 concerning claimant Tanya Marie Anderson (DOB: October 15, 1983; last known address: 4958 Innovation Dr, Apt 406, DeForest, WI 53532), including:
   - Receipt acknowledgment and case/reference number assigned
   - Investigator or adjudicator assignment records
   - Any determination, finding, disposition, or referral generated
   - Internal memoranda or correspondence discussing the report
   - Records of any cross-referral to DHS, DCF, DOJ, or law enforcement

2. All records of my January 2, 2025 inquiry submitted via Contact DWD form (ADM-19735-E) regarding the status of the above fraud report, and any response or internal action taken.

PART B -- FRANCKE / ADJUDICATOR #5139 RECORDS

3. All records created, received, or maintained by Christine Francke and/or Adjudicator "Matt" (ID #5139) in connection with my fraud report filed November 26, 2024 concerning Tanya Marie Anderson, including:
   - All records of the February 21, 2025 evidence submission (BMO bank statements and forensic teller deposit tallies) I provided to Christine Francke
   - Any internal action, review, or disposition resulting from that submission
   - Any decision not to investigate or not to refer the matter, including the identity of the decision-maker and the stated rationale

4. Any Pandemic Unemployment Assistance (PUA) claims filed by or on behalf of Tanya Marie Anderson during 2020-2021, including:
   - Applications and eligibility determinations
   - Weekly certifications
   - Any adjudication, investigation, or review by Francke or Adjudicator #5139
   - Any determination of overpayment or non-overpayment

To the extent such PUA records are disclosable, I request them.

PART C -- CROSS-REFERRAL

5. Any cross-referral to other agencies (DHS, DCF, DOJ, DWD Office of Legal Counsel, or law enforcement) resulting from the fraud report or resulting from the evidence submission.

I am the individual who filed the fraud report and submitted the supporting evidence. I am not requesting confidential UI wage records of third parties but rather the disposition and processing status of my own report, agency correspondence, and referral actions -- all of which are public records.

I request a fee waiver or reduction based on public interest: the underlying conduct involves over $18,575 in potentially fraudulent unemployment payments and was confirmed by a December 20, 2024 DHA Intentional Program Violation finding (FOF-215399, $52,917 concealed income). Disclosure serves the public interest in government accountability.

If any portion of this request is denied, I request a written explanation citing the specific statutory exemption relied upon, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(b).

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(a).

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Waunakee Community School District
905 Bethel Circle
Waunakee, WI 53597

Re: Parental Request for Pupil Records Pursuant to FERPA and Wis. Stat. 118.125(2)

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to my rights as a parent under the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (20 U.S.C. section 1232g) and Wis. Stat. section 118.125(2), I hereby request copies of the following additional pupil records for Liam Alexander Lennon (DOB 03/09/2011):

1. All Section 504 evaluation records, including any assessments, testing results, or screening tools used in the October-December 2024 504 evaluation process.

2. All notes, minutes, and correspondence from the December 20, 2024 Section 504 Plan meeting, including the draft 504 Plan, any proposed accommodations, and records of my objections and the basis for those objections.

3. All records relating to Liam's congenital limb difference (right hand) in WCSD's possession, including any health plans, nurse's office records, physical education accommodations, and communications between school staff regarding Liam's disability.

4. All records of referrals for Child Find evaluation under Section 504 or IDEA from Liam's enrollment date through October 15, 2024, or documentation explaining why no referral was made despite awareness of Liam's visible physical disability.

5. All records of bullying complaints filed through the WCSD online bullying report system regarding Liam Lennon, from September 2024 through the present, including any investigation notes and findings.

This request supplements my prior open records request and is made under my separate parental right to inspect pupil records under FERPA and Wis. Stat. 118.125(2). WCSD must respond within 45 days per FERPA, 34 C.F.R. section 99.10.

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Dane County Department of Human Services
210 Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd., Room 425
Madison, WI 53703

Re: Parental Access Request Pursuant to Wis. Stat. 48.981(7)(a)1.

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to Wis. Stat. section 48.981(7)(a)1., I hereby request access to and copies of all records relating to CPS investigations involving my children:

- Liam Alexander Lennon (DOB 03/09/2011)
- Lincoln Lennon
- Milania Lennon

for the period January 1, 2020 through the present.

I am the biological father and legal custodian of Liam Alexander Lennon. Lincoln and Milania are not my biological or legal children, but I am a subject of the CPS investigations involving them. I request records pertaining to me from those investigations pursuant to Wis. Stat. 48.981(7)(a)1., which provides access to "the subject of a report or record."

Specifically, I request:

1. All CPS intake reports, investigation notes, case notes, and assessment documents for CPS investigations involving the above-named children, including Case ID 8548106 and any related case numbers.

2. All SACWIS contact notes involving CPS workers Heather Stertz and Angela Ivan from May 2023 through the present.

3. All inter-agency communications between Dane County CPS and law enforcement (Waunakee PD, Dane County DA) regarding investigations involving the above-named children.

4. All CPS referrals received from Waunakee Community School District staff regarding the Lennon family, from January 2020 through June 2023.

5. All records relating to the CHIPS proceedings (Case No. 2023JC000068), including any safety plans, protective plans, or placement recommendations.

6. All records of contact between CPS workers and attorneys at Pines Bach LLP (Jordan Loeb, Elisabeth Bach-Van Horn) regarding investigations involving the above-named children.

AUTHORITY: Wis. Stat. 48.981(7)(a)1. provides that "the department, a county department under s. 46.22 or 46.23, or a licensed child welfare agency authorized by the department to provide child welfare services under this section shall, upon request, provide the subject of a report or record made under this section with access to and a copy of any report or record made under this section that pertains to the subject."

Signed confidential information release forms for all family members were submitted on January 1, 2025.

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay.

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Wisconsin Department of Health Services
Office of Inspector General
1 West Wilson Street, Room 950
Madison, WI 53703

Re: Open Records Request Pursuant to Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to Wisconsin's Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. sections 19.31 through 19.39, I hereby request copies of the following records:

1. All records relating to the complaint I filed with the DHS OIG regarding the handling of the SHARES investigation by Jessica Cooper (Dane County), including any intake records, investigation notes, findings, and disposition documents.

2. All records relating to the oversight review I requested on or about February 20, 2025, regarding Jessica Cooper's closure of the SHARES childcare overpayment investigation despite the DHA IPV finding (FOF-215399, $52,917 concealed income).

3. All communications between DHS OIG investigator Angela Dempewolf and Jessica Cooper regarding the SHARES investigation, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

4. Angela Dempewolf's complete investigative file for the FoodShare/SNAP fraud investigation of Tanya Marie Anderson, including the closure rationale dated on or about February 7, 2025.

5. Any communications between DHS OIG and the USDA Office of Inspector General regarding Tanya Marie Anderson's benefit fraud, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

SCOPE: I am the complaining party who filed the fraud reports and provided supporting documentation (including private investigator memo, forensic accounting, and the DHA IPV decision). I am requesting records relating to my own complaints and the institutional response.

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(a).

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Wisconsin Department of Health Services
Office of Inspector General
1 West Wilson Street, Room 950
Madison, WI 53703

Re: Open Records Request Pursuant to Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to Wisconsin's Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. sections 19.31 through 19.39, I hereby request copies of the following records:

1. All records relating to the complaint I filed with the DHS OIG regarding the handling of the SHARES investigation by Jessica Cooper (Dane County), including any intake records, investigation notes, findings, and disposition documents.

2. All records relating to the oversight review I requested on or about February 20, 2025, regarding Jessica Cooper's closure of the SHARES childcare overpayment investigation despite the DHA IPV finding (FOF-215399, $52,917 concealed income).

3. All communications between DHS OIG investigator Angela Dempewolf and Jessica Cooper regarding the SHARES investigation, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

4. Angela Dempewolf's complete investigative file for the FoodShare/SNAP fraud investigation of Tanya Marie Anderson, including the closure rationale dated on or about February 7, 2025.

5. Any communications between DHS OIG and the USDA Office of Inspector General regarding Tanya Marie Anderson's benefit fraud, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

SCOPE: I am the complaining party who filed the fraud reports and provided supporting documentation (including private investigator memo, forensic accounting, and the DHA IPV decision). I am requesting records relating to my own complaints and the institutional response.

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(a).

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

Records Custodian
Wisconsin Department of Children and Families
201 E. Washington Ave.
Madison, WI 53703

Re: Open Records Request Pursuant to Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to Wisconsin's Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. sections 19.31 through 19.39, I hereby request copies of the following records:

1. The complete investigative case file for the Wisconsin SHARES child care subsidy investigation involving Tanya Marie Anderson, Dane County, including all correspondence, memoranda, notes, interview records, and supporting documents. This investigation was referenced in communications between Angela Dempewolf (DHS OIG) and Jessica Cooper (Dane County) and was closed on or about February 20, 2025.

2. Angela Dempewolf's closure memorandum or report regarding the SHARES investigation, including the stated rationale for closing the investigation.

3. All inter-agency communications between DCF, DHS OIG, and the Dane County Department of Human Services regarding the SHARES investigation, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

4. All records reflecting the decision-making process for closing the SHARES investigation, including any supervisory review or approval of the closure decision.

PRIOR REQUEST HISTORY: I submitted a similar request via MuckRock in May 2025. DCF denied the request on or about June 26, 2025 through Attorney Joey Roeth, citing Wis. Stat. 49.83. I submitted signed confidential information release forms for all family members on January 1, 2025. I rebutted the denial on June 27, 2025. I have not received a substantive response to the rebuttal.

This re-filing narrows the request to institutional decision-making records and inter-agency communications, which are not individual case records protected under Wis. Stat. 49.83.

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(a). If this request is again denied, I will seek mandamus relief under Wis. Stat. 19.37(1).

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All records relating to the Wisconsin SHARES child care subsidy investigation handled by Jessica Cooper of the Dane County Department of Human Services, SHARES Division, concerning Tanya Marie Anderson, from January 1, 2024 through the present. This includes Jessica Cooper's investigation notes, emails, memoranda, and any closure documents.

2. All correspondence between Jessica Cooper and Detective Jason Carbon of the Waunakee Police Department from January 1, 2024 through the present, regarding Tanya Marie Anderson, Alexander Lennon, or the Intentional Program Violation finding in DHA Case No. FOF-215399.

3. Jessica Cooper's written explanation or memorandum regarding her determination that there was "no overpayment" in the SHARES investigation, despite the December 20, 2024 DHA decision finding $52,917 in concealed income by clear and convincing evidence.

4. All communications between Jessica Cooper and Angela Dempewolf (DHS Office of Inspector General) regarding the SHARES investigation, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

5. Any supervisory review or quality assurance documents relating to Jessica Cooper's handling of the SHARES investigation.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alexander Lennon
